skill.name: internal-comms
skill.description: Guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code in drafting internal company communications such as 3P updates, newsletters, FAQ answers, status reports, leadership updates, project updates, and incident reports.
skill.summary: Draft structured internal communications using reusable company-style formats.
skill.author: Asmayaseen

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography, reconnaissance, hardcoded URL, and shell backtick patterns, but these are false positives from prose, license text, and Markdown code spans. The main residual risk is that the skill encourages broad review of internal Slack, email, calendar, document, and press sources, which requires careful access control and data minimization.

medium_findings.title: Broad Internal Source Aggregation
medium_findings.description: The skill asks the assistant to use Slack, email, calendar, documents, and external press when drafting company newsletters. This is legitimate for internal communications, but it can expose confidential employee, executive, or company information if permissions are too broad or summaries include unnecessary links.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited guidance explicitly lists several internal and external information sources. There is no evidence of exfiltration, so the concern is data minimization and access governance rather than malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Prose and License Text
low_findings.description: Static alerts for weak cryptographic algorithms are dismissed. The matched lines are ordinary writing guidance, YAML description text, and Apache License terms, not cryptographic code or configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain natural language such as descriptions, disclaimer terms, and communication guidance. No cryptographic primitive, library call, key material, or security-sensitive implementation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern in Writing Guidance
low_findings.description: The duplicate reconnaissance alerts on the newsletter guidance line are dismissed. The line explains first-person company voice and does not instruct system inspection, identity checks, host discovery, or command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact context is copywriting guidance about using we tense. There is no system command, shell syntax, or environment discovery behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Apache License URL
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL alerts are standard Apache License references. They do not cause a network request and are not used by executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both lines are part of the canonical Apache License text. The skill contains no code path that fetches these URLs.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Spans Misread as Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: The backtick execution alerts are dismissed. The backticks wrap example file paths in Markdown documentation and do not invoke Ruby, shell execution, or dynamic commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are Markdown inline-code formatting around examples directory paths. No interpreter, command substitution, or executable script context exists on these lines.

content.value_statement: Teams need clear internal updates that fit the right audience and format. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code draft structured 3P updates, newsletters, FAQs, and leadership communications.
actual_capabilities: Selects guidance for 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQ answers, and general internal messages.
Formats 3P updates with Progress, Plans, and Problems sections.
Drafts company newsletters with sections for announcements, priorities, leadership updates, and social updates.
Creates concise FAQ answers for company-wide employee questions.
Prompts for audience, purpose, tone, and format when the request is unclear.
Encourages links to authoritative internal sources when the user grants access.
limitations: Does not verify company facts unless the user provides sources or connected tools are available.
May need user review before sharing sensitive or executive communications.
Does not enforce access controls for Slack, email, calendar, or document systems.
Does not provide legal, HR, or compliance approval for internal announcements.

use_cases.title: Prepare Weekly Team 3P Updates
use_cases.description: Turn progress notes, plans, and blockers into a short executive-ready update.

use_cases.title: Create Company Newsletter Drafts
use_cases.description: Summarize major announcements, priorities, leadership posts, and social updates for broad employee distribution.

use_cases.title: Answer Employee FAQs
use_cases.description: Convert recurring company questions into clear question-and-answer responses with source references.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic 3P Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a 3P update for the [team name] team covering [dates]. Progress: [notes]. Plans: [notes]. Problems: [notes]. Keep it concise and executive-ready.

prompt_templates.title: Polish a Leadership Update
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with an existing draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this leadership update for clarity, brevity, and company-wide readability. Audience: [audience]. Purpose: [purpose]. Tone: [tone]. Draft: [paste text].

prompt_templates.title: Build an Employee FAQ
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an internal FAQ from these employee questions and source notes. Use one-sentence questions and one-to-two-sentence answers. Flag anything that needs official review.

prompt_templates.title: Synthesize a Company Newsletter
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request using multiple source types
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a company newsletter for [time period] using these announcements, leadership notes, documents, meeting summaries, and press mentions. Group related updates and include only broadly relevant items.

output_examples.input: Draft a weekly 3P update for the mobile team using launch, bug, and staffing notes.
output_examples.output:
Mobile Team (June 22-28)
Progress: Shipped the new onboarding flow and reduced crash reports after the latest patch.
Plans: Focus on release monitoring, analytics review, and the next accessibility fixes.
Problems: Hiring remains the main constraint for the Android backlog.

output_examples.input: Create FAQ answers about a new quarterly planning process.
output: Question: When does quarterly planning start?
Answer: Planning starts next Monday with team-level reviews before executive calibration.
Question: Who should attend the planning meetings?
Answer: Functional leads and directly involved project owners should attend unless their manager assigns a delegate.

output_examples.input: Summarize major company announcements into a newsletter section.
output_examples.output:
Company Announcements
- We opened quarterly planning with a focus on customer reliability and faster enterprise onboarding.
- We published the new hiring plan for priority roles across engineering, sales, and support.
- We shared the updated launch calendar for the next product milestone.

best_practices: Provide source notes, target audience, time period, and desired tone before asking for a draft.
Review generated drafts for confidential details before posting or emailing them.
Use authoritative sources for company-wide announcements and flag uncertain facts.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to invent metrics, decisions, or executive statements without evidence.
Do not include private employee details unless they are approved for broad sharing.
Do not publish drafts without human review for sensitive company topics.

faq.question: What types of internal communications can this skill draft?
faq.answer: It supports 3P updates, newsletters, FAQ answers, status reports, leadership updates, project updates, and incident reports.

faq.question: Does it need access to company systems?
faq.answer: No. It can work from pasted notes, but connected Slack, email, calendar, and document access can improve source coverage.

faq.question: Can it write in a specific company tone?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide audience, purpose, tone, and examples so the draft matches your company style.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential announcements?
faq.answer: Use caution. Review drafts carefully and include only information approved for the intended audience.

faq.question: Does it verify facts automatically?
faq.answer: It does not verify facts unless reliable source material or approved connected tools are available.

faq.question: Who benefits most from this skill?
faq.answer: Internal communications teams, team leads, people operations teams, and executives who need concise employee-facing updates benefit most.
